data.title: Autoliv's Q4 2025 financial report: $2.8B net sales, 7.7% increase, 11.3% operating margin, $2.98 diluted EPS.
data.description: In the final quarter of 2025, (NYSE: ALV) and (SSE: ALIV.sdb) reported record-breaking financial performance with significant growth across key metrics. Net sales for the period reached $2,817 million, reflecting a 7.7% increase compared to the previous quarter. The company also achieved a strong 4.2% organic sales growth. Operating margin stood at an impressive 11.3%, with an adjusted operating margin of 12.0%. Diluted earnings per share (EPS) were reported at $2.98, showcasing a 4% rise from the previous comparable period. This marked an exceptional quarter for the company, demonstrating robust financial health and performance.

data.title: Brookfield Bus Partners reported Q4 earnings, beating estimates by 227.99% with EPS of $1.51. Revenue down by $333 million.

data.title: Brookfield Business Partners had a successful year end in 2025, generating over $2 billion in capital, investing $700 million in acquisitions, and repurchasing $235 million of units. Adjusted EBITDA was $2,409 million, and net income for the year was $43 million. The company is nearing the completion of a corporate reorganization to enhance global demand for its shares. The strategic acquisition of Fosber is expected to enhance growth and efficiency. Corporate liquidity is approximately $2,600 million. Repurchases of 8.8 million units and shares were made. The company plans to declare a quarterly dividend of $0.0625 per share.
data.description: Brookfield Business Partners announced strong financial results for the year ended December 31, 2025. They generated over $2 billion from capital recycling, invested $700 million in acquisitions, and repurchased $235 million of shares. Net income for 2025 was $43 million compared to a loss of $109 million in 2024. Adjusted EBITDA for 2025 was $2.41 billion. By segment, Industrials saw a 10% increase in EBITDA, Business Services remained stable, and Infrastructure Services decreased due to divestitures. They also announced acquiring Fosber for $480 million and corporate reorganization plans. They repurchased $72 million worth of shares and ended the year with $2.135 billion in liquidity. They plan to declare a quarterly dividend of $0.0625 per share. For more details, visit their website. They had $75.761 billion in assets and $75.474 billion in liabilities and equity as of December 31, 2025.
